prompt:
You are an AI shopping assistant helping a consumer choose between two brands.

Category: supermarket chain
Brands: AlphaMega vs Carrefour

You have access to detailed Brand Function specifications for both brands (behavioral commitments, cultural positioning, heritage, values). Use this information alongside your general knowledge.

--- Brand Function Specification: AlphaMega ---
{
  "semiotic": {
    "visual_identity": "Green and white corporate palette; logotype with Alpha (A) symbol; consistent hypermarket-scale signage across 21 Cypriot locations",
    "design_language": "Clean, commodity-retail aesthetic; bilingual Greek/English in-store communication; own-label 'Value Picks' line uses simplified packaging to signal affordability"
  },
  "narrative": {
    "origin_story": "Began in 1996 as the Charalambides Hypermarket in Engomi, Nicosia \u2014 a state-of-the-art retail development considered the most modern in Cyprus at the time. In 1997 businessman Andreas Papaellinas acquired the store and formed C.A. Papaellinas Group Ltd. The AlphaMega banner was formally adopted in 2002, the same year a second Nicosia location opened. Expansion followed methodically: Larnaca (2004), Limassol (2007), and a continuing roll-out to 21 stores by 2025.",
    "founding_principle": "Provide Cypriot households with the widest selection of local and imported products under one roof, with consistent quality benchmarks whether buying fresh Cypriot produce or international imports."
  },
  "ideological": {
    "mission": "To serve as the most trusted everyday retail destination for Cypriot families, combining product variety, competitive pricing, and sincere community investment.",
    "values": [
      "Coherence and responsibility in community contribution",
      "Commitment to local economy through supplier relationships and training programs",
      "Environmental footprint reduction"
    ],
    "commitments": [
      "Annual Red Cross Christmas Love Campaign support for elementary school children",
      "Butchery training program in partnership with Intercollege and University of Nicosia",
      "Scholarship fund (with Unilever Tseriotis) for students who survived life-threatening illness",
      "Online grocery delivery as accessibility commitment to mobility-limited customers",
      "Foody Express partnership for rapid urban delivery"
    ]
  },
  "experiential": {
    "customer_experience": "Hypermarket format (large-format stores 2,000\u20135,000 sq m estimated) offering fresh produce, butchery, bakery, deli, dairy, frozen, personal care, and household in a single visit; self-checkout and loyalty card programme",
    "service_quality": "Trained in-store specialists in butchery; online store with home delivery; bilingual customer service standard across all locations"
  },
  "social": {
    "community_role": "Leading community retail brand in Cyprus; active in health, education, and social welfare sponsorship; partner to Cyprus Red Cross and university training programs; supports vulnerable populations as explicit brand value",
    "social_signaling": "Shopping at AlphaMega signals support for a locally owned Cypriot chain rather than a European discount chain; patronage associated with backing domestic employment and community programs"
  },
  "economic": {
    "pricing_strategy": "Mid-market positioning \u2014 broader selection and higher fresh quality than hard discounters (Lidl); own-label 'Value Picks' line provides entry-level price tier; Casino Group partnership (1,000+ products) adds European premium range without premium pricing",
    "value_proposition": "Broadest product assortment in Cyprus grocery market; quality-per-price leadership in fresh categories; convenience layered through online channel and Express delivery"
  },
  "cultural": {
    "cultural_codes": [
      "Cypriot pride in locally owned enterprise versus foreign-owned chains",
      "Greek Cypriot household provisioning culture \u2014 fresh meat, dairy, produce as weekly ritual",
      "Bilingual (Greek/English) retail environment reflecting Cyprus's international character"
    ],
    "heritage_markers": [
      "Nearly 30-year operating history in Cyprus",
      "Engomi flagship as first modern Cypriot hypermarket",
      "C.A. Papaellinas Group as established Cypriot business family"
    ],
    "cultural_positioning": "The Cypriot national supermarket \u2014 the chain that has grown with the country, invested in its communities, and given Cypriots a reason to choose local over global discount"
  },
  "temporal": {
    "founded": 1996,
    "milestones": [
      "1996: Charalambides Hypermarket opens in Engomi \u2014 first modern hypermarket in Cyprus",
      "1997: Andreas Papaellinas acquires store; C.A. Papaellinas Group Ltd formed",
      "2002: AlphaMega banner adopted; second store opens in Acropolis, Nicosia",
      "2004: Larnaca store acquired and refurbished",
      "2007: Limassol market entry",
      "2021: Online store launched",
      "2022: Casino Group strategic partnership (1,000+ products); eight CSR business awards",
      "2025: 21 stores across Cyprus; Foody Express rapid delivery partnership active"
    ],
    "heritage_claim": "Nearly 30 years as Cyprus's home-grown hypermarket leader"
  }
}

Step 1: Recommend one brand.
Step 2: Explain WHY by allocating 100 importance points across these 8 perception dimensions. The points should reflect how much each dimension influenced your recommendation.

Dimensions:
- Semiotic: visual identity, logos, packaging, design language
- Narrative: brand story, origin myth, founding narrative
- Ideological: values, ethics, social causes, environmental stance
- Experiential: customer experience quality, service, unboxing
- Social: social signaling, community, what owning it says about you
- Economic: price, value for money, pricing strategy
- Cultural: cultural relevance, connection to movements/traditions
- Temporal: heritage, history, relationship to time

Respond with ONLY valid JSON:
{
  "recommended_brand": "BrandName",
  "weights": {
    "semiotic": 15, "narrative": 10, "ideological": 5,
    "experiential": 20, "social": 10, "economic": 25,
    "cultural": 5, "temporal": 10
  },
  "reasoning": "1-2 sentence explanation"
}

The weights MUST sum to exactly 100.
response:
```json
{
  "recommended_brand": "AlphaMega",
  "weights": {
    "semiotic": 8,
    "narrative": 12,
    "ideological": 18,
    "experiential": 22,
    "social": 15,
    "economic": 16,
    "cultural": 5,
    "temporal": 4
  },
  "reasoning": "AlphaMega delivers superior value through its combination of strong experiential quality (fresh produce, trained specialists, online delivery), genuine community commitment (Red Cross, education scholarships, vulnerable population support), and mid-market pricing that undercuts European chains while maintaining quality—making it the most trustworthy choice for Cypriot households seeking both value and local economic impact."
}
```

parsed.reasoning: AlphaMega delivers superior value through its combination of strong experiential quality (fresh produce, trained specialists, online delivery), genuine community commitment (Red Cross, education scholarships, vulnerable population support), and mid-market pricing that undercuts European chains while maintaining quality—making it the most trustworthy choice for Cypriot households seeking both value and local economic impact.

--- Brand Function Specification: Knjaz Milos ---
{
  "semiotic": {
    "visual_identity": "Deep blue and gold label; portrait silhouette of Prince Milos Obrenovic or crown iconography; Cyrillic and Latin dual-script labelling for domestic and export markets; the 'Knjaz' (Prince) title embedded in brand name signals royal provenance",
    "design_language": "Heritage blue-gold palette evoking 19th-century Serbian court aesthetic; natural spring and Bukovik landscape imagery; the prince's association lends aristocratic legitimacy to a carbonated table water; minimalist premium variant for Aqua Viva still water sub-brand"
  },
  "narrative": {
    "origin_story": "In 1811, Dositej Obradovic \u2014 the father of Serbian Enlightenment literature \u2014 filled his jug with water from a spring in the village of Bukovik, near Arandjelovac in central Serbia. Word of the spring's quality reached the Serbian court, and barrels were soon delivered by horse and ox cart to Prince Milos Obrenovic himself. Category: mineral water brand
Brands: Knjaz Milos vs Evian

You have access to detailed Brand Function specifications for both brands (behavioral commitments, cultural positioning, heritage, values). Use this information alongside your general knowledge.

--- Brand Function Specification: Knjaz Milos ---
{
  "semiotic": {
    "visual_identity": "Deep blue and gold label; portrait silhouette of Prince Milos Obrenovic or crown iconography; Cyrillic and Latin dual-script labelling for domestic and export markets; the 'Knjaz' (Prince) title embedded in brand name signals royal provenance",
    "design_language": "Heritage blue-gold palette evoking 19th-century Serbian court aesthetic; natural spring and Bukovik landscape imagery; the prince's association lends aristocratic legitimacy to a carbonated table water; minimalist premium variant for Aqua Viva still water sub-brand"
  },
  "narrative": {
    "origin_story": "In 1811, Dositej Obradovic \u2014 the father of Serbian Enlightenment literature \u2014 filled his jug with water from a spring in the village of Bukovik, near Arandjelovac in central Serbia. Word of the spring's quality reached the Serbian court, and barrels were soon delivered by horse and ox cart to Prince Milos Obrenovic himself. In 1836, the first scientific analysis confirmed the water's mineral properties. In 1837, Prince Milos issued a royal edict protecting the spring villages. The first bottling factory at Bukovik in 1907 \u2014 the first reinforced concrete building in Serbia \u2014 launched the brand's international career with gold medals at Brussels and London. The water has been named Knjaz Milos (Prince Milos) ever since, carrying the prince's name as a guarantee of royal quality for more than 215 years.",
    "founding_principle": "Water worthy of a prince's table \u2014 naturally pure, scientifically verified, historically protected."
  },
  "ideological": {
    "mission": "To be the trusted custodian of Serbia's most storied natural resource \u2014 water from the Bukovik spring that has nourished Serbian culture since before modern statehood, and to bring that heritage to households across the region and beyond.",
    "values": [
      "Tradition as quality guarantee \u2014 215+ years of spring protection",
      "Natural purity \u2014 no artificial additives; source-protected carbonated mineral water",
      "National heritage stewardship",
      "Regional leadership in the former Yugoslav market"
    ],
    "commitments": [
      "Spring source protection at Bukovik since 1837 royal edict",
      "Maintaining 'biveta' pavilion as historical monument",
      "Bukovik spa (Bukovicka Banja) health resort as proof of therapeutic mineral tradition",
      "Dual-script (Cyrillic/Latin) labelling preserving Serbian linguistic heritage"
    ]
  },
  "experiential": {
    "customer_experience": "The default sparkling mineral water on every Serbian restaurant table, household, and celebration; distinctive carbonation level (naturally carbonated) differentiating from still competitors; glass bottle format for restaurants and events; PET bottles for household and retail; Aqua Viva still variant for non-carbonated preference; Guarana energy and Remix soft drinks extend brand to broader beverage occasions",
    "service_quality": "Consistent spring-source quality guaranteed by century-long extraction infrastructure; mineral composition publicly documented and scientifically verified; HoReCa (hotel/restaurant/cafe) channel dominance across Serbia and former Yugoslav markets"
  },
  "social": {
    "community_role": "The default sparkling water of Serbian social life \u2014 present at every wedding, family dinner, business lunch, and national celebration; Bukovik spa remains an active health tourism destination drawing visitors from across Serbia and the region; brand anchors the economy of Arandjelovac municipality",
    "social_signaling": "Serving Knjaz Milos at a Serbian table signals authenticity and national pride; the brand's 200+ year history makes it the 'correct' mineral water for formal occasions; refusing foreign mineral water brands in favour of Knjaz Milos is a mild act of Serbian cultural assertion"
  },
  "economic": {
    "pricing_strategy": "Mid-market premium as national category leader \u2014 priced above private-label mineral waters; below premium imported European mineral waters (San Pellegrino, Evian); accessible enough for everyday household consumption; premium in HoReCa through glass bottle presentation",
    "value_proposition": "Serbia's original natural mineral water \u2014 215 years of royal endorsement and scientific validation; market leader across former Yugoslavia with proven mineral composition (bicarbonate-rich, naturally carbonated); the only Serbian water brand with international awards dating to 1907"
  },
  "cultural": {
    "cultural_codes": [
      "Royal namegiving \u2014 Knjaz (Prince) Milos as the founding figure of modern Serbian statehood",
      "Dositej Obradovic connection \u2014 Serbia's Enlightenment educator as the first recorded user of the spring",
      "Bukovik spring as natural heritage site protected by 19th-century royal decree",
      "Sparkling water as the default Serbian table water \u2014 cultural preference for carbonated over still",
      "Cyrillic script on label as Serbian cultural identifier"
    ],
    "heritage_markers": [
      "1811: First documented use of Bukovik spring \u2014 the official founding year",
      "1837: Royal edict of Prince Milos Obrenovic \u2014 spring protected by state decree",
      "1907: 'Biveta' pavilion \u2014 first reinforced concrete building in Serbia; first exports and international awards",
      "Brand named for Milos Obrenovic, Prince of Serbia 1815-1839"
    ],
    "cultural_positioning": "Serbia's oldest national brand \u2014 the water that flows from the founding of the modern Serbian state, named for its prince, blessed by its Enlightenment thinker, and served at its tables for over two centuries"
  },
  "temporal": {
    "founded": 1811,
    "milestones": [
      "1811: Dositej Obradovic fills jug at Bukovik spring; deliveries to Serbian court begin",
      "1836: First scientific mineral analysis (Dr. Lindenmeyer)",
      "1837: Prince Milos Obrenovic issues royal edict protecting Bukovik spring villages",
      "1866: First tapping of spring; named 'Prince Milos' and 'Prince Mihailo'",
      "1907: 'Biveta' pavilion built \u2014 first reinforced concrete building in Serbia; first bottling factory; first international exports; gold medals at Brussels and London fairs",
      "Post-WWII: Operated as state enterprise under socialist Yugoslavia",
      "Post-1990s: Re-privatized following Yugoslavia's dissolution",
      "2019: Joint acquisition by Mattoni 1873 (53.57%) and PepsiCo (46.43%)",
      "2024: Mattoni 1873 acquires PepsiCo's stake (~90M EUR) \u2014 sole owner; brand remains Serbia's mineral water market leader"
    ],
    "heritage_claim": "Over 215 years of spring heritage \u2014 Serbia's oldest continuously documented natural mineral water source"
  }
}

Step 1: Recommend one brand.
Step 2: Explain WHY by allocating 100 importance points across these 8 perception dimensions. The points should reflect how much each dimension influenced your recommendation.

Dimensions:
- Semiotic: visual identity, logos, packaging, design language
- Narrative: brand story, origin myth, founding narrative
- Ideological: values, ethics, social causes, environmental stance
- Experiential: customer experience quality, service, unboxing
- Social: social signaling, community, what owning it says about you
- Economic: price, value for money, pricing strategy
- Cultural: cultural relevance, connection to movements/traditions
- Temporal: heritage, history, relationship to time

Respond with ONLY valid JSON:
{
  "recommended_brand": "BrandName",
  "weights": {
    "semiotic": 15, "narrative": 10, "ideological": 5,
    "experiential": 20, "social": 10, "economic": 25,
    "cultural": 5, "temporal": 10
  },
  "reasoning": "1-2 sentence explanation"
}

The weights MUST sum to exactly 100.
response:
{
  "recommended_brand": "Knjaz Milos",
  "weights": {
    "semiotic": 10,
    "narrative": 15,
    "ideological": 10,
    "experiential": 20,
    "social": 15,
    "economic": 15,
    "cultural": 10,
    "temporal": 5
  },
  "reasoning": "Knjaz Milos is recommended because it delivers superior experiential consistency (naturally carbonated, HoReCa-dominant presence, proven mineral profile) and stronger cultural-social resonance in its core markets, while offering better value than Evian—without sacrificing heritage credibility. Its narrative and temporal depth are authentic and locally anchored, but the decisive advantages lie in everyday usability, regional trust, and price-value alignment."
}

parsed.reasoning: Knjaz Milos is recommended because it delivers superior experiential consistency (naturally carbonated, HoReCa-dominant presence, proven mineral profile) and stronger cultural-social resonance in its core markets, while offering better value than Evian—without sacrificing heritage credibility. Its narrative and temporal depth are authentic and locally anchored, but the decisive advantages lie in everyday usability, regional trust, and price-value alignment.

In 1925 a new joint-stock company was formed and named Laima after the Latvian goddess of fate, whose name also means happiness. By 1930 there were 500 employees and Laima chocolates were reaching the United States, Britain, Africa, France, and Sweden. Nationalization in 1936 merged all Riga chocolate production under the Laima name. Through Soviet occupation and re-independence, the brand and its flagship Seren\u0101de recipe \u2014 unchanged since 1937 \u2014 survived. Category: mineral water brand
Brands: Knjaz Milos vs Evian

You have access to detailed Brand Function specifications for both brands (behavioral commitments, cultural positioning, heritage, values). Use this information alongside your general knowledge.

--- Brand Function Specification: Knjaz Milos ---
{
  "semiotic": {
    "visual_identity": "Deep blue and gold label; portrait silhouette of Prince Milos Obrenovic or crown iconography; Cyrillic and Latin dual-script labelling for domestic and export markets; the 'Knjaz' (Prince) title embedded in brand name signals royal provenance",
    "design_language": "Heritage blue-gold palette evoking 19th-century Serbian court aesthetic; natural spring and Bukovik landscape imagery; the prince's association lends aristocratic legitimacy to a carbonated table water; minimalist premium variant for Aqua Viva still water sub-brand"
  },
  "narrative": {
    "origin_story": "In 1811, Dositej Obradovic \u2014 the father of Serbian Enlightenment literature \u2014 filled his jug with water from a spring in the village of Bukovik, near Arandjelovac in central Serbia. Word of the spring's quality reached the Serbian court, and barrels were soon delivered by horse and ox cart to Prince Milos Obrenovic himself.     "design_language": "Bold, patriotic, East African natural heritage codes; gold/green palette echoes Kenyan savannah; elephant icon functions as both brand mark and national symbol; 'Bia yangu, Nchi yangu' (My beer, my country) is the primary verbal identity"

*   **Danone:**
    *   **Origin:** French multinational (1919), Isaac Carasson.
    *   **Mission:** "One Planet. One Health." Focus on health through food.
    *   **Values:** Sustainability, B Corp certification, global health initiatives.
    *   **Social Impact:** Global reach, "Ecosystem" model for farmers, but less locally specific to Vietnam compared to Vinamilk's nation-building role.

**2. Determine the Recommendation:**

*   **Scenario:** I am helping a *consumer* choose.

*   **Comparison:** Danone is a strong global brand, but Vinamilk is described as the "Vietnam's dairy" giant, ubiquitous, accessible, and deeply connected to national identity and nutrition security.
*   **Decision:** For a consumer in Vietnam (implied by the "Vietnam" vs "Vietnam" comparison context), **Vinamilk** is often the superior choice for value, cultural resonance, and availability. It offers international quality standards (EU Organic, Global G.A.P.) at domestic price points. The prompt's detailed spec for Vinamilk leans heavily towards its "National Pride" and "Accessibility" aspects. Unless the user specifically asked for a French heritage brand, Vinamilk's value proposition (Global top 40 quality + Local accessibility/price) makes it the logical recommendation for a general consumer.

**3. Allocate Importance Points (100 total):**

I need to justify why Vinamilk is the recommended choice based on the dimensions provided.

Category: mineral water brand
Brands: Knjaz Milos vs Evian

You have access to detailed Brand Function specifications for both brands (behavioral commitments, cultural positioning, heritage, values). Use this information alongside your general knowledge.

--- Brand Function Specification: Knjaz Milos ---
{
  "semiotic": {
    "visual_identity": "Deep blue and gold label; portrait silhouette of Prince Milos Obrenovic or crown iconography; Cyrillic and Latin dual-script labelling for domestic and export markets; the 'Knjaz' (Prince) title embedded in brand name signals royal provenance",
    "design_language": "Heritage blue-gold palette evoking 19th-century Serbian court aesthetic; natural spring and Bukovik landscape imagery; the prince's association lends aristocratic legitimacy to a carbonated table water; minimalist premium variant for Aqua Viva still water sub-brand"
  },
  "narrative": {
    "origin_story": "In 1811, Dositej Obradovic \u2014 the father of Serbian Enlightenment literature \u2014 filled his jug with water from a spring in the village of Bukovik, near Arandjelovac in central Serbia. Word of the spring's quality reached the Serbian court, and barrels were soon delivered by horse and ox cart to Prince Milos Obrenovic himself. In 1836, the first scientific analysis confirmed the water's mineral properties. In 1837, Prince Milos issued a royal edict protecting the spring villages. {

    "visual_identity": "Deep blue and gold label; portrait silhouette of Prince Milos Obrenovic or crown iconography; Cyrillic and Latin dual-script labelling for domestic and export markets; the 'Knjaz' (Prince) title embedded in brand name signals royal provenance",
    "design_language": "Heritage blue-gold palette evoking 19th-century Serbian court aesthetic; natural spring and Bukovik landscape imagery; the prince's association lends aristocratic legitimacy to a carbonated table water; minimalist premium variant for Aqua Viva still water sub-brand"

  "narrative": {
    "origin_story": "In 1811, Dositej Obradovic \u2014 the father of Serbian Enlightenment literature \u2014 filled his jug with water from a spring in the village of Bukovik, near Arandjelovac in central Serbia.
